memory.summary: My team is stressed about the project deadline, but I'm too relaxed to help. I should push them to start now instead of doing it myself later.
Teammate A is panicking about the deadline, but I'm too relaxed to help. I should push them to start now instead of doing it myself later.

The afternoon is slipping away and I'm still at the start, which is unfair to my teammates who are already panicking. I need to stop being so relaxed and push them to start now instead of doing it myself later.
My teammates are spiraling while I'm coasting, and that imbalance is getting heavier. I need to stop overthinking it and just push them to start now.
Teammate A is spiraling, and my relaxation feels selfish. I need to stop coasting and demand they start now before I feel guilty.
My teammates are spiraling and I'm still coasting, which feels selfish. I need to demand they start now instead of overthinking it myself.

desires: Stop feeling guilty about coasting and just demand my teammates start working on the term project right now before I spiral further.
Get that trivial chore done instantly so I can stop overthinking it and actually relax without feeling selfish.
Not have to explain myself or negotiate boundaries with my cohort again because they keep spiraling while I try to manage everything.

goals.description: Send a direct, unfiltered text to teammate_A demanding immediate execution of the technical plan; no more guessing or coasting.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value is screaming that seeing them flail makes me feel guilty and threatens our group cohesion. My assertiveness axis demands I cut through their panic loop right now rather than letting them spiral further. If I don't set this hard boundary today, they'll keep coasting and I'll end up doing everything myself anyway, which is unsustainable for us.

goals.description: Finish the trivial chore instantly so I can stop overthinking it and actually relax without feeling selfish.
goals.rationale: I only have 6 energy points left and two hours of sleep debt. My impulsive time horizon means I'm discounting long-term productivity gains; forcing this now would just make me irritable and ruin the meal with my close friends. But wait—my current desire is specifically about not feeling exhausted *tomorrow* even if it means pushing stress through now. So while I might defer the *social* aspect (the meal), the core goal of finishing the task shifts from 'for the sake of stability' to 'for the sake of tomorrow's peace.' I'll drop the immediate execution to save energy but mark it as active pending tomorrow night because procrastination bias is high.

agent.last_reasoning: My affiliation value screams that seeing them flail makes me feel guilty and threatens our group cohesion. If I don't set this hard boundary today, they'll keep coasting and I'll end up doing everything myself anyway, which is unsustainable for us.

goals.description: Spend the next hour with cohort_3 before they leave, explicitly stating that I need to decompress and won't be available for project updates until tomorrow morning.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) outweighs task outcomes here; protecting my close friend group's emotional state is non-negotiable, even if it delays our progress on the term project.

goals.description: Execute quick_chore immediately after the social block ends to get that instant win feeling without stressing about long-term consequences.
goals.rationale: I'm too emotionally invested in the social moment right now to force this trivial task; I'll only do it once the immediate stress of being alone passes.

goals.description: Set a hard boundary with teammate_A regarding the term project: 'We are done for today, start early tomorrow so we don't both burn out.'
goals.rationale: This feels like a negotiation to happen naturally during the sleep-deprived morning rather than forcing it now when I'm already defensive.

desires: Make sure the team doesn't blame me for dragging our feet on that project, even if it means pushing them to start now and risking their stress levels.
Get some actual sleep tonight so I don't crash tomorrow, even though my brain is screaming at me to do this trivial chore right now.
Spend the next hour with cohort_3 before they all go home; I can't handle being alone while everyone else is stressed out.

goals.description: Call teammate_A right now to shut down their panic loop and explicitly state that I am taking over the technical lead because they are doing everything wrong; demand they stop guessing and start executing my plan immediately.

goals.rationale: I can't let my energy tank alone; being isolated makes me feel guilty and defensive. However, since I just decided to dominate the conversation with teammate_A, I need to physically escape this mental spiral before I lose all patience for social interaction entirely.
